category_title: Verbal pupils who ignore or protest adult-led interactions
minimal_context: The student is participating in a 1:1 playtime session with a classroom adult using blocks and balls.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time for our 1:1 playtime session. We are going to play with the blocks and balls.

STUDENT: Blocks. (Asks for the blocks fairly fast to meet her own needs)

TUTOR: Here are the blocks. Now, stack the blocks up high.

STUDENT: (Ignores the adult's directive and plays with the blocks on her own, showing no interest in interacting)

TUTOR: Look at the ball. Roll the ball to me.

STUDENT: No! (Protests the directive communication, acting wilful and determined to have her own way)

options.A: Record the data in scatter plots and ABC charts when the playtime session finishes.
options.B: Use instruments as figurative media, such as images, posters, blocks, and balls.
options.C: Utilize regulated settings to acquire skills before transitioning to a classroom setting with peers.
options.D: Use facilitative strategies and communication temptations during the 1:1 playtime session.
